instruction.title: State Isolation & Render Boundaries
instruction.overview: This specification outlines state scopes, render boundaries, and performance optimization patterns to keep Interaction to Next Paint (INP) under 80ms.

variable.name: activeCurrency
variable.limits: Renders only within target pricing card elements.

variable.name: billingCycle
variable.limits: Renders only inside pricing card cost digits text nodes.

variable.name: hoverCoordinates
variable.limits: Re-renders mouse coordinates inside active bento cell.

composition_rules.rule: Pass static server elements (RSCs) as children props to interactive client shells (RCCs). This allows React to skip re-rendering child sections when container states change.
Event handlers passed down to dropdown inputs must use useCallback hooks to maintain reference stability.

hover_interactions.rule: Standard card hovers, button scale transformations, and border glows must use pure CSS :hover selectors.
GPU compositor threads handle CSS hovers without running JS threads or triggering React virtual-DOM cycles.
hover_interactions.exception: Observability 3D tilts must use local mouse event listeners restricted to the target card container.

debounced_observers.rule: Resize observer triggers must use passive callbacks debounced to a minimum frequency of 100ms.
Observers must initialize on client mounts and disconnect on unmounts to prevent memory leaks.

derived_states.rule: Avoid setting state inside useEffect hooks in response to other state changes.
Derive dynamic strings (annual discount rate displays, locale formatted prices) inline during render passes.

scroll_locking.rule: Opening the mobile menu drawer locks the document body scrolling.
Managed via a state-isolated hook that toggles overflow: hidden on the document element during mount/unmount lifecycles.
